data.title: Trump pardons Binance founder Changpeng “CZ” Zhao after 11 months of deliberation
data.description:
Donald Trump has granted a full pardon to Changpeng “CZ” Zhao, the founder of Binance, ending almost a year of political back-and-forth over what is the most-watched case in crypto. The decision came after over eleven months of lobbying, private meetings, and direct appeals that finally convinced the “crypto president” to sign the pardon on […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trump-pardons-binance-founder-cz/

data.title: BNB Chain’s paid messaging platform, ReachMe, permanently shut down
data.description:
A paid texting app called ReachMe, which was part of the BNB Chain, has announced its closure on its website. In addition, all user data has been managed securely as per the privacy policy. Changpeng Zhao (CZ), CEO of Binance, was one of many notable crypto industry individuals whom users could privately chat on ReachMe. […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/bnb-chains-paid-messaging-platform-reachme-permanently-shut-down/

data.title: U.S. President Trump Pardons Binance Founder and Former CEO, CZ
data.description: According to an exclusive from the Wall Street Journal, U.S. President Trump has pardoned Binance founder and former CEO, Changpeng Zhao (CZ).

data.title: Crypto Industry Mergers Surpass $10 Billion in Third Quarter
data.description: According to PANews, the third quarter of 2025 saw a significant milestone in the cryptocurrency industry, with mergers and acquisitions exceeding $10 billion for the first time. This marks a more than 30-fold increase compared to the same period last year, as reported by Architect Partners. A notable transaction was FalconX's acquisition of 21shares, highlighting a broader trend of cryptocurrency experts entering traditional investment channels through regulated products. U.S. President Donald Trump's policies have spurred a wave of mergers and acquisitions, altering strategic considerations for companies like 21shares. As regulatory barriers ease, major Wall Street firms are increasingly engaging with the cryptocurrency sector, prompting existing companies to build competitive defenses.

data.title: JPMorgan predicts that the average gold price will exceed $5,000 in 2026 and is expected to reach $6,000 in the long term.
data.description: JPMorgan analysts maintained their bullish stance on gold on Thursday, predicting that the price will average $5,055 per ounce by the fourth quarter of 2026. The bank's research note stated that this forecast assumes that quarterly investor demand and central bank gold purchases will average 566 tons in 2026. Natasha Kaneva, JPMorgan's head of global commodities strategy, stated, "Gold remains our highest-conviction long position this year, and as the market enters a Fed rate-cutting cycle, gold prices have room to rise." Gregory Shearer, head of base and precious metals strategy, added, "The Fed's rate-cutting cycle, coupled with concerns about stagflation, concerns about Fed independence, and the risk of currency devaluation, all create a favorable backdrop for gold." Analysts believe the recent market consolidation is healthy. Kaneva said, "The pullback reflects the market digesting the rapid gains since August. ... It's normal to feel nervous in the face of such a rapid rise. This is essentially a supply-demand imbalance—buyers are plentiful and sellers are scarce." She reiterated her long-term target of $6,000 per ounce by 2028, emphasizing that gold's trajectory should be viewed from a multi-year perspective. (Gold Ten)

data.title: BNB Chain and YZI Labs jointly launch the BNB Hack: Local Series hackathon and registration is now open
data.description: Registration is now open for the BNB Hack: Local Series hackathon, jointly launched by BNB Chain and YZI Labs. Winners will have the opportunity to receive support from the $1 billion Builder Fund. Winning teams will receive prizes and receive: direct interview opportunities for the MVB program; EASY Residency incubation status; up to $50,000 in ecosystem launch support; and exposure on the BNB Chain network.
